moduleLogs.subtitle: Current module: {{module}}. Logs are paginated from the module's own runtime log database.
moduleLogs.fetchFailed: Failed to load module runtime logs
moduleLogs.fetchFailedWithStatus: Failed to load module runtime logs (HTTP {{status}})

dashboard.title: Pro Mode Realtime Command Center
dashboard.subtitle: Observe live brain snapshots, cognitive tool status, and transcript events through the FastAPI aggregation layer.
dashboard.runtimeDegraded: The runtime is currently operating in degraded mode.
dashboard.backendDisconnected: Unable to connect to the Zentex backend. Check service status.
dashboard.sectionLoadFailed: Some dashboard sections failed to load:
dashboard.streamParseError: Failed to parse the realtime event stream.
dashboard.loading: Pulling realtime state from the Zentex backend...
dashboard.focusFallback: The backend did not return working-memory state.
dashboard.noConflicts: There are no unresolved cognitive conflicts.
dashboard.criticalConflictWarning: A critical cognitive conflict was detected. The system has switched to conservative degradation and paused expansion reasoning.
dashboard.noPlugins: No plugin state is currently exposed through the web aggregation layer.
dashboard.noEvents: No events match the current filter.
dashboard.weightFallbackWarning: Weight drift or audit rejection detected. Safely rolled back to the conservative default profile.
dashboard.noInteractionMind: No interaction-mind state is currently available.
dashboard.interactionMindWarning: Misunderstanding risk detected. Prioritize intent clarification.

plugins.title: Plugin & Cognitive Tool Management
plugins.subtitle: Inspect live CognitiveToolRegistry state for manual acceptance of lifecycle transitions and failure counts.
plugins.backendError: Unable to connect to the Zentex backend. Check service status.
plugins.noBoundPlugin: No plugin is currently bound to this feature. This path has not actually taken control yet.
plugins.noVersions: No plugin versions are currently registered for this feature.
plugins.officialOnlyHint: Only official-release plugins can be force-enabled
plugins.protectedDeleteHint: System default plugins cannot be deleted
plugins.forceDisableHint: Only active plugins can be force-disabled
plugins.actionSuccessForceEnableWithAutoDisableP: Plugin force-enabled and conflicting active plugins were auto-disabled:
plugins.actionSuccessForceDisable: Plugin force-disabled and fallback was checked
plugins.overrideDialogBody: This feature does not support multiple active plugins. Force-enabling will automatically disable the currently active version.
plugins.empty: No cognitive tools match the current filters.

audit.title: Audit & Replay
audit.subtitle: Inspect model-provider request/response traces and how the nine-question flow assembled them.
audit.backendError: Failed to load model-provider audit traces. Check backend status.
audit.empty: No model-provider traces match the current filters.

agents.title: Agent Asset Management Center
agents.subtitle: Assets & Task Pipeline
agents.pageFunctionHelp: This page shows external Agent assets actually registered in the backend. Registration writes to the Agent asset table; lists, Q3 inventory, task dispatch, receipts, and audit chains all use the same registration data.
agents.deleteConfirm: Delete Agent registration "{{name}}"? This removes it from the backend registry.
agents.stepProfileHelp: Define the Agent identity, display name, version, and capability description used by task routing and audit views.
agents.stepSecurityHelp: Configure the real endpoint, auth token, role, and scope Zentex uses to call this Agent. Tokens are treated as sensitive values.
agents.stepHandshakeHelp: Final registration enters the real handshake and capability probe flow. Failures appear in status and audit records.
agents.techNameHelp: Unique internal Zentex ID. Backend generation may be available, but a stable explicit ID is recommended.
agents.humanNameHelp: Name shown in pages and the task center.
agents.versionHelp: Agent capability or protocol version, for example 1.0.0.
agents.funcDescHelp: Describe task types, boundaries, and limitations. Task routing reads this description.
agents.endpointHelp: Real HTTP/Webhook entrypoint Zentex calls for this Agent.
agents.authTokenHelp: Token used when calling the Agent. It must not appear in plaintext audit output, task remarks, or page data.
agents.roleTagHelp: Used for filtering and task assignment, for example worker, reviewer, planner.
agents.scopeHelp: Business scope this Agent is allowed to handle. Multiple scopes can be expanded by backend support later.
agents.auditWarning: Agent will be created in PENDING state until Cloud Audit completes.
agents.handshakeInfo: Initiating handshake will perform connectivity and capability probes.
agents.validationError: Validation Failed: Name, version, and description are required.

dialogs.retryMessage: Retry task "{{title}}"? It will be reset to todo.
dialogs.cancelWarning: Cancelled tasks cannot be restored automatically.
dialogs.retryInfo: Retry resets the task to todo for re-execution.

nineQuestions.subtitle: 9-Question overview list, supporting drill-down from real brain cache to details and independent sandbox testing.
nineQuestions.label: Question Label
nineQuestions.name: Question Name
nineQuestions.initializingTitle: Brain Cold Start: Executing Full 9-Question Deduction...
nineQuestions.initializingMessage: Building initial cognitive snapshot, please wait.
nineQuestions.forceRunSuccess: Forced execution of a full 9-question cycle completed. List refreshed.
nineQuestions.sandboxSubtitle: Inject Mock Context on the left, view local preprocessed evidence and final inference in the isolated sandbox on the right. This page only calls the sandbox interface and will not overwrite main brain cache or write to formal event streams.

q1.title: Q1: Physical & Environmental Audit
q1.loadingSnapshot: Pulling Q1 snapshot from production environment...
q1.actionRunDeduction: Please run a complete 9-question deduction process first, then refresh this page to view the results.
q1.noInferenceResult: Q1 has not produced inference results yet
q1.actionTriggerFullDeduction: The deduction snapshot for this question is empty. Please trigger a full 9-question deduction in Zentex Brain Runtime and return to this page after completion.
q1.checkRuntimeStatus: NineQuestionState is not mounted to the runtime. Please check the startup status of Zentex Brain Runtime and ensure the service is correctly initialized before refreshing.
q1.checkNetworkOrDevServer: Unable to connect to the backend service. Please check your network connection or ensure the dev server is running and try again.
q1.retryHint: Please refresh the page to retry. If the problem persists, check the backend service logs for more details.
q1.noRecord: Q1 has no inference records yet. Please run the complete 9-question deduction process before viewing this page.
q1.noStructuredEvidence: No structured evidence data available. The current session may not have completed Q1 deduction yet. Please run the 9-question process first.

q4.subtitle: Capability boundary and actionable-space audit (independent API GET /nine-questions/q4)
q4.noActiveSession: No readable nine-question snapshot is available yet
q4.actionRunDeduction: Run the complete nine-question process first, then refresh this monitoring page.
q4.noInferenceResult: Q4 has not produced a capability-boundary snapshot yet
q4.actionTriggerFullDeduction: The capability-boundary audit may be blocked by Q1-Q3 prerequisites or may not have been triggered. Run the full nine-question flow again.
q4.engineNotReady: Backend deduction engine is not ready
q4.checkRuntimeStatus: NineQuestionState is not mounted. Confirm Zentex Brain Runtime has initialized before refreshing.
q4.retryHint: Check the network, backend service, and Q4 partition endpoints, then refresh.
q4.authPartial: Q4 authenticity status: degraded or partially failed
q4.fallbackUsed: This run used fallback data and should not be treated as a complete capability boundary.
q4.noFallbackUsed: This run did not use fallback data.
q4.partialData: Q4 only loaded partial partition data, so the page is showing the available result.
q4.noStructuredEvidenceLayout: No structured evidence is currently available. This area keeps the layout and shows a recoverable empty state.
q4.noStructuredCapabilityEvidence: No structured capability evidence is available.
q4.loadRawFailed: Failed to load Q4 raw payload
q4.basePartitionsFailed: All Q4 base partitions failed to load, so the page context cannot be built.

tasks.tabInfo: Tasks are grouped by execution state. Open details to review description, executor, dependencies, and workflow information.

garbageAnalysis.title: Garbage & Duplicate Task Analysis
garbageAnalysis.subtitle: Rule screening covers idempotency, zombie, orphan, stale, and dependency-loop signals; semantic merging requires an explicit LLM scoring run.
garbageAnalysis.llmTooltip: Calls the LLM only for suspected semantic duplicate groups; unavailable LLMs do not produce fake semantic scores.
garbageAnalysis.noFindings: No duplicate or garbage tasks found.

exceptionReasons.csvInspectionCapability: CSV file format and timestamp inspection
exceptionReasons.designatedCapabilityUnavailableWithExecu: The selected executor {{executor}} cannot perform {{capability}}. The task is paused until an executor with that capability is selected or enabled.
exceptionReasons.designatedCapabilityUnavailable: The selected executor cannot perform {{capability}}. The task is paused until an executor with that capability is selected or enabled.
exceptionReasons.connectorCapabilityNotSpecified: An external connector was selected, but no concrete business operation was selected. The task is paused until the execution capability is specified.
exceptionReasons.healthProbeNotBusinessExecutable: The selected connector capability only checks connectivity and cannot perform this business task. The task is paused until a real business execution capability is selected.
exceptionReasons.designatedOwnerUnavailable: The selected executor is not registered, enabled, or available. The task is paused until an available executor is provided.
exceptionReasons.noExecutorForCapability: No available executor supports {{capability}}. The task is paused until a matching executor is registered or enabled.
exceptionReasons.resourceGap: Missing {{capability}}. The task is paused until that resource is available.

tasks.deleteConfirm: Delete this task? This removes it from the task database.
tasks.archiveConfirm: Archive this task? It will remain visible in the Archived tab.
tasks.archiveRemarks: Archived from the task detail page while preserving task-center queryability

verificationStatuses.internalConfigured: Internal verification is configured, but no displayable Q9 verification instruction is available
verificationStatuses.enabledNoVerifier: Verification enabled, but no real verifier is configured

tasks.title: Title
tasks.workflowSubtitle: Node inputs, outputs, and runtime state for task {{taskId}}.
tasks.workflowNoSelectedNode: Select a node to inspect details
tasks.noReadableMetadata: This metadata has no directly displayable key fields. Expand raw metadata for troubleshooting.

logs.moduleSubtitle: Paginated task behavior audit records from task_audit_log; this is not the module runtime log entry.

mcp.deleteConfirm: Delete MCP Server registration "{{name}}"? This removes the server from the backend registry.
mcp.warningDesc: Risk of suspension, immediate optimization required
mcp.factorFailureCount: Task failure count: Each failure deducts 5 points
mcp.factorTimeoutRate: Response timeout rate: Each timeout deducts 3 points
mcp.factorHumanIntervention: Human intervention frequency: Each manual takeover deducts 10 points
mcp.resetDaily: Daily Reset: Scores automatically recover at 00:00 UTC
mcp.resetRecovery: Performance Recovery: Successful task execution gradually restores credit

cli.assetManagementSubtitle: Manage external command-line tool assets based on the principle of 'complete separation of cognition and execution'.
cli.pageFunctionHelp: This page shows CLI tools actually registered in the backend. Registration and the table use the same CLI registry; test calls execute the real command and write results, errors, auth injection, and audit trace back to the system.
cli.formToolNameHelp: Internal Zentex identifier, for example gemini, pytest, or playwright-cli.
cli.formCommandExecutableHelp: Enter only the executable name or absolute path, not a full shell command line.
cli.formDescriptionHelp: Describe what this CLI can do. Task routing and audit views use this description.
cli.registerBasicsHelp: For a normal global CLI, fill only tool name, executable command, function description, and read-only/execution permission. CLIs that require installation must be installed on the computer running the backend first; registration verifies the command is executable. Project and documentation fields are optional advanced metadata.
cli.playwrightCliExampleHelp: Prefills the tool name, executable, description, and documentation URLs for microsoft/playwright-cli. The computer running the backend must have Node.js 18+, npm, and @playwright/cli@latest installed first.
cli.playwrightCliInstallHint: If it is installed but not registered yet, filling playwright-cli and registering it is the recommended path; the backend verifies the command during registration.
cli.alreadyRegistered: CLI tool "{{name}}" is already registered in Zentex. Use the table to test, activate, disable, or delete it before rebuilding.
cli.commandShellLineError: Enter only an executable name or absolute path here, not a full shell command with spaces. Provide arguments in test calls.
cli.registerErrorAlreadyRegistered: This CLI tool is already registered. Use another tool name or delete the existing registration before registering again.
cli.registerErrorShellLine: Invalid executable command: enter only an executable name or absolute path, not a full shell command line.
cli.registerErrorCommandMissing: The backend cannot find or run "{{command}}". If it is installed, make sure the backend process PATH can resolve it; otherwise install it before registering.
cli.registerErrorReadOnlyMutating: This command appears to mutate system state and cannot be registered as read-only. Uncheck read-only or use a read-only command.
cli.advancedSettingsHelp: Use these only when the CLI depends on a fixed working directory, project display metadata, or documentation learning.
cli.formProjectPathPlaceholder: e.g., <project-path>; leave blank for global CLIs
cli.formProjectPathHelp: Used only when a call does not specify a working directory. Global CLIs such as gemini or curl usually leave this blank.
cli.formProjectNameHelp: Display and identification metadata only. It does not affect command execution.
cli.formProjectDescriptionHelp: Optional context about the project or use case. It does not affect command execution.
cli.formHelpDocUrlHelp: Used for documentation learning and detail views. Leave blank when no official docs are needed.
cli.formProjectDocUrlHelp: Project-level docs used to understand what this CLI is for in the target project, beyond generic --help output.
cli.alertReadOnly: [Cognitive Assist] Read-only operations only. The system will prohibit any state modifications.
cli.alertReadWrite: [Physical Execution] Operations with side effects. Subject to SafetyGate review and CloudAudit auditing.
cli.authSettingsHelp: Save an API key or configure pre-call login here. Secrets go to the credential vault; registration stores only credential_ref.
cli.authNoInteractiveLogin: Only non-interactive login commands are supported. QR code, MFA, captcha, and browser-interactive login are not supported.
cli.credentialIdHelp: Leave blank to auto-generate. Registration references this ID and never stores plaintext secrets.
cli.authEnvNameHelp: CLI default is ZENTEX_CLI_API_KEY. For Gemini CLI, use GEMINI_API_KEY.
cli.apiKeySecretHelp: Submitted only to the encrypted credential vault; not written to CLI env metadata, command line, or audit logs.
cli.testArgumentsHelp: Split into real command arguments by spaces. Do not put API keys, passwords, or tokens in arguments.
cli.testStdinInputHelp: Use only for CLIs that need stdin. Secrets should be injected from the credential vault, not typed here.
cli.testWorkingDirectoryHelp: Used only for this test call. Leave blank to use the registered default working directory or system default.
cli.deleteConfirm: Delete CLI tool registration "{{name}}"? This removes it from the backend registry.
cli.title: Title

externalConnectors.title: External Application Connector Center
externalConnectors.subtitle: Manage Office, file apps, browser SaaS, enterprise systems, local services, and automation bridge connectors independently.
externalConnectors.pageFunctionHelp: This page shows external connectors actually registered in the backend. Registration writes to the connector registry; health checks, real test-calls, Q3 inventory, and audit chains read the same data.
externalConnectors.name: Name
externalConnectors.deleteConfirm: Delete external connector registration "{{name}}"? This removes it from the backend registry.
externalConnectors.registerBasicsHelp: Enter the connector identity, target app, and optional base path. Registration stores connector metadata only; real capabilities come from manifests or later capability configuration.
externalConnectors.connectorIdHelp: Unique Zentex ID used by task routing, Q3 inventory, and audit correlation.
externalConnectors.typeHelp: Choose the connector runtime shape: API, desktop app, browser app, file app, local service bridge, or SDK.
externalConnectors.targetAppHelp: Identifier of the real application or system, for example office.word, browser.chrome, jira.cloud.
externalConnectors.nameHelp: Name shown in pages and the task center.
externalConnectors.descriptionHelp: Describe what system this connector can access, what it can do, and its limits.
externalConnectors.basePathHelp: Default root path for file or local-service connectors. Leave blank when no fixed path is needed.
externalConnectors.testCallHelp: This calls the backend test-call endpoint, not a frontend fake result. Fill arguments according to the connector's real capability.
externalConnectors.capabilityHelp: Capability name to execute, for example read_document, write_file, export_pdf.
externalConnectors.filePathHelp: Real input path or target resource path passed to the connector.
externalConnectors.writeContentHelp: Used by write capabilities. Leave blank for read-only calls.
externalConnectors.outputPathHelp: Output path for export or generation capabilities.
externalConnectors.fetchFailed: Failed to load external application connectors
externalConnectors.manifestFetchFailed: Failed to load external connector knowledge cards

memory.title: Memory Governance
memory.subtitle: Manage enhanced memory status, trust, correction notes, source links, and audit records.
memory.partialApiFailure: Some API calls failed. Data may be incomplete:

errors.pageLoadUnexpected: Unexpected page load error. Check the browser console logs.
errors.searchFailed: Enhanced memory search failed. Check backend status.
errors.managementFailed: Memory management action failed. Check backend status.
errors.forceAutoOrganizeFailed: Failed to force-start automatic memory organization

repair.noRepairAllItems: This repair_all run did not find records that need handling.

search.title: Enhanced Memory Search
search.label: Search experiences, failure lessons, and version chains
search.emptyHint: Enter keywords to search semantic / procedural / episodic memory.

records.title: Memory Records
records.noMatches: No records match the current filters.

detail.title: Memory Details
detail.selectHint: Select a memory record to view source links, audits, and governance actions.

audit.title: Audit Records
audit.empty: This memory does not have extra governance audits yet.

upgrades.title: Upgrade Management
upgrades.subtitle: View and manage the full lifecycle status of LLM upgrades, plugin evolutions, and plugin creations.
upgrades.currentFilterStats: Current Filter Stats: all {{all}} / waiting {{waiting}} / ongoing {{ongoing}} / completed {{completed}} / failed {{failed}} / cancelled {{cancelled}}
upgrades.noRecordsInCategory: No upgrade records in this category.
upgrades.cleanupReasonPrompt: Enter reason for cleaning up failed candidate

learning.title: Controlled Learning Dashboard
learning.subtitle: Manage and monitor the system's autonomous learning cycles.
learning.forceAutoOrganizeFailed: Failed to force-start learning auto organization

workspaces.title: Workspace Management
workspaces.subtitle: Manage readable local directories. A workspace maps to a directory and does not bind to a role.
workspaces.permissionHelp: When a workspace is added, the backend checks that the local path exists, is a directory, and is at least readable.
workspaces.name: Workspace Name
workspaces.pathHelp: Must be a local directory on the backend host, readable by the running service process.
workspaces.forbiddenActions: Forbidden Actions (What Should NOT Be Done)
workspaces.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this workspace?

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Set the system role, default workspace constraints, and default task configuration.
settings.noWorkspaces: No workspaces are available yet. The role can be configured first; workspaces are local directories managed separately.
settings.defaultWorkspaceHelp: After saving, the selected workspace becomes the system default. Workspace configuration stores directory-related constraints only, not the role.
settings.roleSectionHelp: Define the single system role. It maps to Q2 "Who am I" and affects later 9Q processing. If set, Q2 will not replace it with the computed role; Q2 only compares both roles with LLM judgement and displays the result.
settings.rolePlaceholder: For example: Full-stack Engineer, Code Reviewer, Product Analyst
settings.roleHelp: This is the system role, not a workspace field.
settings.roleDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this role owns, what it is good at, and what it should prioritize.
settings.coreValuesHelp: One item per line. These are read as Q2 identity boundaries and later 9Q context.
settings.defaultConfigHelp: These values become execution context for tasks, memory, and the nine-question flow.
settings.forbiddenActions: Prohibited Items (What Must Not Be Done)
settings.forbiddenActionsPlaceholder: For example: delete production data, modify credentials, bypass tests
settings.taskGoalsHelp: These goals are saved on the default workspace and passed into Q8 objective generation as extra goals.
settings.taskGoalsPlaceholder: For example: prioritize a real verification loop

test.title: Test Page
test.subtitle: Lists all LLM-integrated features available for testing, supporting manual parameter injection and output observation.
test.llmStatusCheckFailed: LLM status check failed, please check the backend.
test.catalogLoadFailed: Failed to load model feature test catalog, please check backend status.
test.historyLoadFailed: Failed to load history records for this feature.
test.runLogLoadFailed: Failed to load detailed log for this test record.
test.llmDisabled: LLM is disabled, all test operations are prohibited.
test.noHistoryRecords: No history records for this feature yet.
test.interventionSubtitle: Verify that manual intervention write-back, idempotency, and replay display are correct.
test.interventionDescription: Execute intervention process tests here, such as replay deduplication when repeating idempotency_key.
test.pluginLifecycleSubtitle: Verify that plugin start/stop, degradation, and rollback paths behave as expected.
test.pluginLifecycleDescription: Execute plugin management related tests here, such as state consistency checks after forced enable/disable.
test.traceReplaySubtitle: Verify that Trace aggregation, event replay, and context summaries are consistent.
test.traceReplayDescription: Execute trace-link related tests here, such as pulling by trace_id and checking event order and key fields.
